w:r.w:t: ZYTRAK
Global Healthcare Procurement — Built on Stellar
Master Platform Overview · Version 2.0
Blockchain-powered procurement infrastructure for Africa's healthcare supply chain — operating globally on Stellar
What this document is
This is the master overview of everything Zytrak is — updated to reflect the full Stellar blockchain integration. It replaces every earlier brief as the single definitive description of the platform.

It covers: the redefined platform vision, what Stellar enables that was not possible before, the complete product and module architecture, the innovation framework, the revenue model, and the global expansion roadmap.
Every team member, investor, sprint judge, and partner should read this document first.
Section
What it covers
01 — The new platform identity
What Zytrak is now, how Stellar changes the thesis entirely
02 — Why Stellar, precisely
The specific Stellar capabilities powering each layer of the platform
03 — The full product architecture
Six modules: IMS, Marketplace, Logistics, Stellar Finance, Intelligence, Compliance
04 — How Stellar is used — technically
Horizon, Soroban smart contracts, USDC, anchors, provenance records
05 — Organisation types and users
All healthcare facilities, supplier tiers, and user roles
06 — The innovation framework
What makes Zytrak globally defensible and fundable
07 — Revenue model
All seven revenue streams across the 3-year roadmap
08 — Global expansion model
Nigeria first, Africa-wide by Year 2, global emerging markets Year 3
09 — Sprint deliverables
What gets built and demonstrated in the 5-day Stellar sprint
10 — Stellar ecosystem alignment
How Zytrak fits into the Stellar network's 2026 strategic priorities
01. The new platform identity
Zytrak — one sentence

Zytrak is the world's first blockchain-native healthcare procurement platform: a full-stack operating system where African hospitals, laboratories, and clinics manage their medical supply inventories, source from verified global suppliers, and settle payments — domestic and international — on the Stellar network.

Before the Stellar integration, Zytrak was a well-designed B2B procurement marketplace for Nigerian healthcare. After it, Zytrak is something categorically different: a platform where every transaction is settled on a global financial network trusted by Franklin Templeton, PayPal, Visa, MoneyGram, and the United Nations. The supply chain records are permanent and tamper-proof. The payments cross borders in 3–5 seconds. The escrow is not a policy — it is code.
1.1 The three things Stellar changes about Zytrak
Without Stellar
With Stellar
Local payments only — labs cannot pay international manufacturers
Cross-border settlement in 3–5 seconds via USDC on Stellar — labs pay Chinese, German, and US suppliers as easily as paying a Lagos distributor
Escrow depends on trusting Zytrak
Soroban smart contracts hold payment — neither Zytrak, lab, nor supplier can manipulate it. The code is the escrow.
Supply chain records stored in a mutable database

Every key event — supplier certification, order confirmation, cold chain receipt — hashed and written permanently to the Stellar blockchain. Independently verifiable by NAFDAC, ISO auditors, and global health funders.
Revenue limited to Nigerian NGN transactions
Platform earns on every cross-border USDC transaction across the entire Stellar anchor network — 90+ countries, 475,000+ on/off-ramp locations
Fundable as a Nigerian startup
Fundable as global financial infrastructure built on a network trusted by PayPal, Franklin Templeton, Visa, and the Marshall Islands government
1.2 The positioning statement

Zytrak sits at the exact intersection of three of the biggest global trends in 2026: the digitisation of African healthcare supply chains ($3B+ market), the institutional adoption of Stellar as payment and settlement infrastructure (Franklin Templeton, PayPal, Visa, MoneyGram already live), and the rise of stablecoins as the default cross-border payment rail for emerging markets (Nigeria has the highest stablecoin adoption rate in the world at 11.9% of the population). These trends were each independently powerful. Zytrak is the product that sits exactly where all three intersect.
The global comparables — where Zytrak is heading
Zipline (logistics infrastructure for African healthcare) — valued at billions, backed by the US State Department with $150M for Africa expansion.
MedSource Kenya (digital healthcare procurement marketplace East Africa) — raised $220M. Zytrak is the West Africa equivalent with a blockchain-native financial layer that MedSource does not have.
OmniRetail Nigeria (B2B distribution with embedded finance) — raised $20M Series A, reached profitability 2024. Same marketplace-to-financial-infrastructure playbook.

The differentiation: none of these three are built on blockchain rails. Zytrak is. That means Zytrak can operate in any country on the Stellar network from day one — not after years of local banking partnerships.
02. Why Stellar — precisely

This section is the answer to every investor, judge, and partner who asks: 'Why Stellar and not Ethereum, Solana, or a traditional fintech stack?' The answer is specific, technical, and grounded in what Stellar does that nothing else does for this exact use case.
Capability
What Stellar offers
Why it matters for Zytrak specifically
Settlement speed
3–5 second finality. Confirmed transaction in the time it takes to blink.
A Nigerian lab confirming delivery should release supplier payment instantly — not in 3 business days. Healthcare supply chains cannot tolerate bank settlement delays.
Transaction cost
$0.0007 average per transaction. Fractions of a cent.
Zytrak processes thousands of inventory receipts, order confirmations, and payment events daily. At $0.0007 each, the cost of blockchain operations is effectively zero at any scale.
USDC native issuance
USDC is natively issued on Stellar by Circle. Not bridged. Not wrapped. Native.
Labs pay suppliers in USDC without FX risk or bank intermediaries. Suppliers receive USDC and off-ramp via their local anchor. No CBN FX restrictions apply to the Stellar leg of the transaction.
Anchor network
475,000+ cash-to-crypto on/off-ramp locations in 90+ countries.

Every verified supplier in any country can receive payment in their local currency via an anchor. Labs in Nigeria, Kenya, Ghana, or Egypt all settle the same way. This is the infrastructure that makes global expansion trivial.
Soroban smart contracts
Rust-compiled WebAssembly contracts with predictable, low fees and no gas spikes.
The escrow contract holding supplier payment is written in Rust — a memory-safe language with the strongest security guarantees in systems programming. No Solidity exploits. No gas auction wars.
Compliance at protocol level
Built-in KYC flows, jurisdiction-aware asset controls, freeze and clawback on assets.
NAFDAC, CBN, and international health funders require documented procurement compliance. Stellar's compliance primitives are native — not bolted on after the fact.
Yellow Card anchor (Africa)
Yellow Card: the only licensed stablecoin on/off-ramp across 20 African countries on Stellar.
The NGN-to-USDC conversion for every international order goes through Yellow Card — already live, already licensed, already on Stellar. This is not a future plan. It exists today.
Real-world asset infrastructure
Franklin Templeton issued $580M+ in tokenised US Treasuries on Stellar. RWA market crossed $1B on-chain in January 2026.

Future Zytrak financial products — equipment leasing backed by tokenised assets, yield on lab credit balances — can be built on infrastructure that already holds over a billion dollars of institutional real-world assets.
Energy efficiency
Stellar uses Federated Byzantine Agreement — no mining, no proof-of-work, minimal energy.

Healthcare organisations and their global health funders have ESG requirements. A platform built on an energy-efficient blockchain can be promoted to WHO, USAID, and Gates Foundation without the reputational risk of proof-of-work chains.
Developer experience
JavaScript SDK (@stellar/stellar-sdk), Rust SDK (soroban-sdk), Stellar CLI, testnet with Friendbot funding, Lab explorer, comprehensive docs.
David can write and deploy the escrow contract in Rust on the testnet in 1–2 days. The JavaScript SDK integrates with the existing Node.js backend without a new infrastructure stack.
03. The full product architecture — six modules

Zytrak is structured as six integrated modules sharing a single data layer, a single authentication system, and a single Stellar integration layer. The Stellar layer is not a separate product — it runs underneath modules 1 through 6 like plumbing. Users see inventory management, ordering, and payments. The blockchain is the infrastructure they never have to think about.
The core loop — how the six modules work together
IMS tells you what you have and what you are running low on.
Marketplace surfaces what you need from verified global suppliers, with pricing and verification data.
Logistics moves it from supplier to your facility with full cold chain documentation.
Stellar Finance settles the payment: Paystack for domestic NGN, Soroban escrow + USDC for international.
Provenance writes an immutable blockchain record for every significant event across all four modules above.
Intelligence learns from every transaction — forecasting demand, scoring suppliers, predicting stockouts.
The loop is complete: every cycle makes the inventory smarter, the procurement faster, and the data richer.
Module 1 — Inventory Management System (IMS)
What it is

A perpetual, real-time inventory management system for every healthcare facility on the platform. Labs, hospitals, clinics, diagnostic centres, blood banks, and research institutions all manage their full stock here — reagents, consumables, equipment, pharmaceuticals, PPE.
What it does
Real-time stock levels across all locations within an organisation (main lab, branch, blood bank ward, pharmacy, storage)
Lot tracking with NAFDAC number, batch number, manufacture date, expiry date — every received lot is a traceable record
FEFO enforcement (First Expired, First Out) — the system surfaces the earliest-expiring lot first for consumption
Expiry alerts at 90 days (informational), 30 days (warning), 7 days (critical) — displayed on dashboard homepage
Reorder point management — configurable minimum and maximum stock levels per product per location
Automated reorder suggestion — when stock hits reorder point, the system pre-builds an order in the marketplace
Stock adjustments with mandatory reason code — every adjustment is logged immutably
Inter-location stock transfers with full approval and receipt workflow
Stocktaking (physical count) with variance reconciliation
Goods receipt logging — captures lot details, temperature at receipt for cold chain items, supplier PO reference
Bulk import from CSV/Excel — allows facilities to onboard their existing inventory data in hours
Stellar connection

Every goods receipt — the moment a lab logs that a physical delivery has arrived and been accepted — triggers a provenance record written to the Stellar blockchain. This creates an immutable chain of custody: this lot, from this supplier, received at this temperature, at this time. NAFDAC inspectors and ISO auditors can verify it independently.
Module 2 — Procurement Marketplace
What it is

A curated, verified global catalogue where healthcare facilities find and order supplies from local Nigerian distributors, regional African suppliers, and international manufacturers — all in one place, with pricing, verification badges, and real-time stock status.
What it does
Product catalogue across four tiers: reagents and diagnostics, consumables and disposables, equipment and instruments, supplies and PPE
Universal search: by product name, generic name, brand, NAFDAC number, supplier, or product code
Advanced filters: temperature profile, supplier location, in-stock only, price range, delivery time, verification badge
Product comparison: up to 4 products side by side — price, NAFDAC number, temperature profile, supplier badge, delivery time
Verified supplier badges: NAFDAC licence, ISO certifications (13485 for devices), performance score all visible
Request for Quotation (RFQ) for equipment and bulk orders — multiple suppliers respond competitively
Multi-supplier cart with single payment — backend creates sub-orders per supplier, buyer sees one total
Approval workflow for large orders — configurable threshold routes order to procurement officer for sign-off
Tiered pricing: standard, volume (10+), contract (50+)
One-click repeat ordering from order history
Stellar connection

Supplier verification is a Stellar event. When the admin approves a supplier, a transaction is written to the Stellar blockchain with a hash of their NAFDAC licence number, certification details, and approval timestamp. This makes supplier credentials immutable and independently verifiable — not just stored in Zytrak's database.
Module 3 — Order and Logistics Management
What it is

The complete order lifecycle management system: from the moment a lab places an order to the moment they confirm delivery and the supplier receives payment. Ten distinct order statuses with tracked transitions, cold chain documentation, and dispute resolution.
Order lifecycle — ten states
Status
What it means
Pending payment
Order created, Paystack or Stellar payment initiated, supplier not yet notified
Paid
Payment confirmed and held in escrow. Supplier notified. Fulfilment SLA clock starts.
Confirmed
Supplier confirms stock availability and commits to fulfil within lead time
Packed
Supplier marks goods as packed and ready for dispatch
Dispatched
Waybill number logged. Cold chain departure data recorded. Lab receives tracking info.
In transit
Order is moving. Real-time tracking via logistics API where available.
Delivered
Logistics partner marks delivered, or lab confirms receipt
Confirmed good
Lab confirms products are in acceptable condition. Escrow released to supplier. IMS stock updated.
Disputed
Lab has raised a quality or delivery issue. Escrow held. Zytrak ops reviews within 24 hours.
Cancelled
Pre-payment cancellation, or post-dispute cancellation with refund triggered
Cold chain management

Every temperature-sensitive product (refrigerated 2–8°C, frozen −20°C, ultra-cold −80°C) has a mandatory chain of custody record: supplier storage temperature at dispatch, packaging method, transit time, and temperature at receipt logged by the lab. If the temperature at receipt is outside range, the lot is flagged as breached and cannot be added to usable stock without admin review. This cold chain record is also written to the Stellar blockchain as a provenance event.
Logistics network — three tiers
Tier 1 Nigeria: GIG Logistics, Sendbox, Kwik Delivery (ambient). Cold chain van service in Lagos, Abuja, Port Harcourt initially.
Tier 2 West and East Africa: Aramex Africa, Ethiopian Airlines Cargo, DHL Express for cross-border regional.
Tier 3 International: DHL Supply Chain for equipment. Biocair for ultra-cold biological materials.
Module 4 — Stellar Financial Layer
What it is — the most important architectural distinction

This is where Zytrak diverges fundamentally from every other procurement platform in the region. The financial layer is not a payments bolt-on. It is a Stellar-native financial infrastructure layer that handles domestic settlement, cross-border settlement, programmable escrow, and — in later phases — embedded lending and asset-backed leasing. All of it runs on the Stellar network.
Payment rail 1 — Domestic NGN: Paystack

For transactions between Nigerian labs and Nigerian suppliers, Paystack remains the optimal rail. Instant NGN processing, familiar UX, Paystack subaccounts with manual settlement used as the escrow mechanism. Zero complexity for users. Commission split happens at transaction time. Supplier share held until delivery confirmation.
Payment rail 2 — Cross-border: Stellar + USDC + Yellow Card

For international orders (Chinese manufacturers, German equipment suppliers, US reagent companies), payment routes through Stellar. The lab's NGN is converted to USDC by Yellow Card — the only licensed stablecoin on/off-ramp across 20 African countries, already live on Stellar. USDC is deposited into a Soroban escrow smart contract on the Stellar network. When the lab confirms delivery, the smart contract releases USDC directly to the supplier's Stellar account. The supplier off-ramps to their local currency via their country's Stellar anchor. Total settlement time: under 10 seconds. Cost: Yellow Card spread (0.5–1%) plus Stellar network fee ($0.0007).
Soroban escrow smart contract

The escrow smart contract is the trust mechanism that makes unfamiliar parties transact with confidence. A new international supplier joining Zytrak does not need to trust that Zytrak will release their payment. The Soroban contract on the Stellar blockchain holds the USDC, and it releases automatically when the delivery confirmation event is posted. The code is the escrow. No human intermediary. No policy document. No 'trust us'.
Financial products — phased
Phase
Product
Description
MVP
ZytrakPay — domestic
Paystack subaccount escrow. Supplier paid within 2 hours of confirmed dispatch for verified suppliers.
MVP
ZytrakPay — international
Soroban smart contract escrow. USDC via Yellow Card. 3–5 second settlement after lab confirmation.
Month 6
ZytrakCredit — Lab BNPL
Net-30 credit for labs with 6+ months history. 2.5% fee. Funded from Zytrak float. Labs order more, faster.
Month 9
ZytrakStock — Supplier finance
Purchase order advance to suppliers who cannot pre-fund large orders. Recovered from settlement. 2–3% fee.
Year 2
ZytrakLease — Equipment leasing
Zytrak purchases equipment from global manufacturers, leases to facilities at monthly payments over 36 months.
Year 2
ZytrakProtect — Supply insurance
Supply disruption insurance. Premium per lab per month. Partner underwriter. First of its kind for African labs.
Module 5 — Supply Chain Provenance (Stellar blockchain layer)
What it is

The immutable, public audit trail of every significant supply chain event — written to the Stellar blockchain, retrievable via a Stellar Explorer URL, permanently verifiable by any party without access to Zytrak's internal database.
Events written to Stellar
Event
What is hashed and stored
Who verifies it
Supplier approval
Organisation ID, NAFDAC licence number, certifications hash, approval timestamp, admin ID
NAFDAC, procurement auditors, global health funders
Purchase order confirmed
Order ID, buyer org, supplier org, product SKUs, quantities, total value, payment reference
Audit committees, accreditation bodies
Cold chain departure
Order ID, storage temperature, packaging method, dispatch timestamp, courier, sensor serial
ISO 15189 auditors, cold chain inspectors
Goods receipt
Order ID, lot numbers, expiry dates, temperature at receipt, receiving user, timestamp
Regulatory bodies, internal quality teams
Dispute resolution
Order ID, dispute reason, evidence hash, resolution, resolving admin, timestamp
Legal, insurance, regulators
Supplier certification renewal
NAFDAC number, new expiry, renewal document hash, Zytrak admin confirmation
NAFDAC, international buyers
The Stellar Explorer link

Every provenance record generates a URL to the Stellar blockchain explorer. Labs, facilities, auditors, and regulators can independently verify any record without logging into Zytrak or trusting Zytrak's database. This is what distinguishes a blockchain-native platform from a database with a blockchain logo on it.
Module 6 — Intelligence and Analytics
What it is

The platform's learning layer. Every inventory transaction, every order, every payment, every delivery, every cold chain record, and every provenance hash feeds a growing intelligence system. Over time, this system can predict what a lab needs before the lab knows it needs it.
Procurement spend dashboard: total spend by month, category, supplier, location. Year-on-year trend.
Stockout tracker: days per month each product had zero stock. The ROI metric that sells Zytrak to every CFO.
Consumption rate analysis: average daily usage per product, seasonal patterns, projected stockout date.
Reorder optimisation: AI-suggested reorder points based on 3-month consumption history and supplier lead times.
Price intelligence: real-time price comparison across all Zytrak suppliers. Labs can see if they are overpaying.
Supplier performance scoring: fill rate, on-time delivery, cold chain compliance, dispute rate. Per supplier.
Demand forecasting: platform-wide demand signals by product and region — published as the Zytrak Healthcare Supply Index.
Stellar transaction analytics: cross-border payment volume, USDC settlement trends, anchor performance.
04. How Stellar is used — technically
4.1 New files in the codebase — src/modules/stellar/
File
Purpose
stellar.routes.js
GET /stellar/verify/:entityId (public — returns on-chain record and explorer URL). GET /stellar/escrow/:orderId (returns escrow contract status).
stellar.controller.js
Reads req params, calls stellar.service functions, returns structured responses.
stellar.service.js

writeProvenanceRecord(eventType, entityId, dataPayload): hashes payload, submits Stellar transaction with manageData operation. verifyRecord(entityId): queries Stellar Horizon API for the stored hash. getEscrowStatus(orderId): calls Soroban contract get_status(). initiateEscrowDeposit(orderId, amount, buyerKey, supplierKey): calls Soroban contract deposit(). releaseEscrow(orderId): calls Soroban contract release(). refundEscrow(orderId): calls Soroban contract refund().
stellar.helpers.js

buildDataHash(payload): SHA-256 hash of JSON-stringified event data. buildExplorerUrl(txHash, network): returns testnet or mainnet Stellar Explorer URL. formatStellarAmount(amount): converts to Stellar stroops.
escrow.contract/
Rust crate containing the Soroban escrow contract. Compiled to WASM and deployed via Stellar CLI. Source: src/contract.rs, test: src/test.rs, Cargo.toml, Makefile.
4.2 Environment variables added
Variable
Description
STELLAR_NETWORK
testnet (during sprint) or public (mainnet post-sprint)
STELLAR_HORIZON_URL
https://horizon-testnet.stellar.org (testnet) or https://horizon.stellar.org
STELLAR_RPC_URL
Soroban RPC endpoint — required for smart contract interactions
ZYTRAK_STELLAR_PUBLIC_KEY
Zytrak platform Stellar account public key — signs provenance transactions
ZYTRAK_STELLAR_SECRET_KEY
Zytrak platform Stellar account secret key — NEVER commit, never expose
ESCROW_CONTRACT_ID
The deployed Soroban escrow contract ID on testnet/mainnet
YELLOW_CARD_API_KEY
Yellow Card API key for NGN-to-USDC conversion (post-sprint)
4.3 Escrow contract — Soroban Rust (structure)
#![no_std]
use soroban_sdk::{contract, contractimpl, contracttype, token, Address, Env, Symbol};
#[contracttype] pub enum DataKey { Escrow(Symbol) }
#[contracttype] pub struct EscrowData { pub lab: Address, pub supplier: Address,
pub amount: i128, pub token: Address, pub status: Symbol }
#[contract] pub struct ZytrakEscrow;
#[contractimpl] impl ZytrakEscrow {
// Lab deposits USDC into this contract — called when order is placed
pub fn deposit(env: Env, order_id: Symbol, lab: Address, supplier: Address,
token: Address, amount: i128) {
lab.require_auth();
token::Client::new(&env, &token).transfer(&lab, &env.current_contract_address(), &amount);
env.storage().persistent().set(&DataKey::Escrow(order_id.clone()),
&EscrowData { lab, supplier, amount, token, status: Symbol::new(&env, "pending") });
// Called by Zytrak backend when lab confirms delivery
pub fn release(env: Env, order_id: Symbol, caller: Address) {
caller.require_auth(); // Only platform account can call
let data: EscrowData = env.storage().persistent().get(&DataKey::Escrow(order_id)).unwrap();
token::Client::new(&env, &data.token).transfer(&env.current_contract_address(), &data.supplier, &data.amount);
// Called for disputes — returns funds to lab
pub fn refund(env: Env, order_id: Symbol, caller: Address) {
caller.require_auth();
let data: EscrowData = env.storage().persistent().get(&DataKey::Escrow(order_id)).unwrap();
token::Client::new(&env, &data.token).transfer(&env.current_contract_address(), &data.lab, &data.amount);
05. Organisation types and user roles
5.1 Buyer organisations — healthcare facilities
Organisation type
Primary supply need
Stellar-specific benefit
Medical laboratory (private)
Reagents, stains, culture media, rapid test kits, consumables
Provenance records for ISO 15189 accreditation. International reagent suppliers payable in USDC.
Hospital laboratory
Lab reagents + coordination with pharmacy and clinical departments
Department-level provenance. Multi-location cold chain documentation.
Diagnostic centre
Broad: lab reagents, imaging consumables, PPE, calibration standards
Equipment procurement internationally via Soroban escrow. RFQ responses in USDC.
Private hospital / clinic
Pharmaceuticals, consumables, PPE, surgical supplies, equipment
Cross-border equipment procurement. Supply chain audit trail for JCI/ISO accreditation.
Public hospital / government
All of the above + full regulatory compliance and audit trails
Immutable blockchain procurement records for public sector audit and anti-corruption requirements.
Research institution
Specialised reagents, molecular biology supplies, lab instruments
International supplier access, cold chain USDC payment, biological material traceability on-chain.
Blood bank
Blood bags, anticoagulants, reagents, cold chain equipment
Strict temperature and lot traceability on Stellar. Every received unit has an immutable record.
Veterinary clinic / lab
Veterinary reagents, consumables, instruments
Same full IMS and marketplace as human healthcare, with dedicated product categories.
5.2 Supplier tiers
Supplier tier
Who they are
Payment settlement
Tier 1 — Nigerian distributor
NAFDAC-registered Nigerian distributors of reagents and consumables
Paystack NGN → Paystack subaccount escrow → NGN payout on confirmation
Tier 2 — Nigerian manufacturer
Local manufacturers of consumables, glassware, PPE (CAC + NAFDAC)
Paystack NGN → Paystack subaccount escrow → NGN payout on confirmation
Tier 3 — Regional African
Distributors in Ghana, Kenya, South Africa, Rwanda
Paystack or PAPSS → local currency via regional anchor → settlement in local currency
Tier 4 — Global manufacturer/OEM
Chinese, German, Indian, US equipment and reagent manufacturers
Yellow Card NGN→USDC → Soroban escrow contract → USDC released to supplier's Stellar account → supplier off-ramps via local anchor
Tier 5 — Equipment service provider
Calibration, maintenance, and repair companies
Service contracts managed through platform. Invoice-based payment on service completion confirmation.
06. The innovation framework — why this is globally defensible

Innovation is not a feature. It is the set of structural advantages that make Zytrak impossible to copy quickly, regardless of how much money a competitor raises. Zytrak has five structural advantages.
Innovation pillar
Why it is structurally defensible
Blockchain-native supply chain provenance

Every competitor's audit trail is in a mutable database. Zytrak's is on the Stellar blockchain. You cannot replicate this retroactively — the chain of custody has to start from day one. Every month Zytrak operates, the blockchain record grows longer and more valuable.
Stellar cross-border rails

The existing alternatives for Nigerian labs paying international suppliers are SWIFT (3–7 days, 3–5% fees, often blocked by CBN) and nothing. Zytrak's Stellar integration, via Yellow Card's existing licensed anchor network, creates a payment capability that no Nigerian healthcare procurement platform currently has.
Proprietary transaction data as credit infrastructure

After 12 months, Zytrak holds the world's only real transaction dataset on Nigerian healthcare supply chain behaviour: supplier reliability, demand patterns, stockout frequency, cold chain compliance. This data is a proprietary credit score for a sector that has never been formally banked. Banks and DFIs will pay to access it.
Clinical outcome connection

Zytrak tracks stockout days per lab per product. When stockouts fall from 14 days/month to 2 days/month after joining Zytrak, that delta translates directly to tests performed, diagnoses made, and patients treated. No other procurement platform in Africa can demonstrate this connection. It is what moves global health funders — Gavi, WHO, Gates Foundation, USAID — from 'interesting' to 'fund this'.
Network effects that compound

Every new supplier adds pricing competition and product range. Every new lab order adds demand signal intelligence. Every transaction improves the credit model. Every Stellar provenance record grows the audit trail. The platform gets more valuable for every participant the more other participants use it. This is the architecture that justifies premium valuations.
07. Revenue model — seven streams across three years
Timeline
Product
Model
Scale estimate
Now
Transaction commission
8–12% of every order processed
₦50M GMV/month → ₦4–6M monthly
Month 3
Logistics margin
12–20% markup on delivery cost
Every order needs delivery — meaningful at scale
Month 6
Lab subscription
₦15K–₦80K/month per facility
100 subscribers at ₦30K = ₦3M MRR
Month 6
ZytrakCredit — Lab BNPL
2.5% fee on Net-30 invoices
₦100M BNPL book = ₦2.5M monthly interest
Month 9
Supplier financing referral
0.5–1% of financed orders via lender partner
Pure data-enabled revenue, no capital needed
Year 2
ZytrakLease — Equipment leasing
35% total return over 36 months per asset
₦200M leasing portfolio = ₦6M+ monthly
Year 2
Market intelligence data
Annual data licences to pharma, NGOs, governments
$10K–$100K per client. 10 clients = $500K annual
The Stellar revenue multiplier

Every cross-border transaction via Stellar creates an additional revenue event: Yellow Card FX spread (shared), Stellar transaction fee (negligible), and Zytrak cross-border commission (1–2% on top of standard commission).

As Zytrak expands to Ghana, Kenya, Ethiopia, and Egypt — all countries with active Stellar anchors — every new market is accessible without a new banking partnership. The anchor network is already there.
This is the revenue architecture that makes Zytrak a global business, not just a Nigerian one.
08. Global expansion model
Phase
Geography
What enables it
Phase 1 — Year 1
Nigeria (Lagos, Abuja, Port Harcourt, Ibadan, Kano)
Paystack domestic rails + Stellar provenance + Soroban escrow for international orders. Full product suite.
Phase 2 — Year 2
Ghana, Kenya, South Africa, Rwanda
Stellar anchor network covers all four countries natively. USDC settles in GHS, KES, ZAR, RWF. No new banking setup required — anchor already exists.
Phase 3 — Year 3
Ethiopia, Egypt, Côte d'Ivoire, Senegal, Tanzania
Same Stellar anchor network. PAPSS for regional African settlements. Zytrak becomes the pan-African standard.
Phase 4 — Year 4+
South and Southeast Asia (India, Bangladesh, Philippines, Indonesia)
Yellow Card operates in India and the Philippines. Stellar anchors active. Healthcare supply chains face identical problems. Same product, new geography.
Why Stellar makes global expansion structurally cheap
Traditional fintech expansion requires: local banking partnerships (6–18 months each), local payment gateway contracts, local FX compliance, and local regulatory approvals for money movement.

Zytrak's expansion requires: a Stellar anchor in the target country (already exists in 90+ countries), local NAFDAC or equivalent supplier verification rules (documented by Zytrak ops), and local language support (UI localisation).
The financial rails are already built. Expanding to a new country is a product and operations problem, not a payment infrastructure problem. This is what 'built on Stellar' actually means.
09. Five-day sprint deliverables

The sprint runs on Stellar Testnet throughout. All architecture is identical to mainnet. Real money is not involved. The deliverables below represent what judges will see — a fully working demonstration of every Stellar capability integrated into a real product solving a real healthcare problem.
Day
Build target
Demo output
Stellar provenance service (stellar.service.js)

Every key event in the live demo writes to Stellar Testnet. Show 'View on Stellar' buttons across supplier profiles, orders, and goods receipts. Open Stellar Testnet Explorer — real transaction, real hash, real immutable record.
Soroban escrow contract in Rust — deployed to testnet
Contract is live at a known address on Stellar Testnet. Call get_status() and show the contract is deployed, callable, and responding.
Node.js ↔ Soroban contract integration

Place an international demo order. Watch USDC deposit appear in the contract via Testnet Explorer. Confirm delivery. Watch USDC release to supplier's Stellar address. The whole flow on-screen in real time.
Frontend — blockchain UI layer
'View on Stellar' on every relevant screen. International payment option showing USDC flow. Blockchain badge on verified suppliers. Stellar transaction history on the order detail screen.
End-to-end demo polishing

Full demo run: register lab → approve supplier (provenance to chain) → place order (escrow funded) → confirm delivery (escrow released) → view entire on-chain history for the transaction. Clean, fast, real.
10. Stellar ecosystem alignment — why this matters for funding

Zytrak is not using Stellar as a credential. Zytrak is building exactly the kind of platform that the Stellar Development Foundation is actively investing in, partnering with, and supporting through grants and the Enterprise Fund.
Stellar's 2026 strategic priority
How Zytrak advances it
Accelerating asset adoption and cross-border use

Zytrak creates real USDC transaction volume on Stellar — not speculative, not DeFi, but healthcare B2B procurement payments. This is exactly the real-world stablecoin usage that Stellar's strategy is built around.
Enabling seamless enterprise and institutional adoption

Hospitals and laboratories are institutional healthcare buyers. Zytrak brings them onto Stellar without requiring any blockchain knowledge. They see an inventory and procurement tool. The Stellar network is invisible infrastructure underneath.
Advancing core network capabilities in payments

Zytrak demonstrates Soroban escrow for B2B supplier payments and Horizon-based provenance records — two concrete, high-value smart contract use cases that strengthen the Soroban ecosystem's real-world proof points.
Financial inclusion via USDC in emerging markets

Nigerian labs, particularly smaller private labs in secondary cities, have no current way to pay international manufacturers. Zytrak gives them access to global supply markets via Yellow Card + Stellar USDC. This is financial inclusion with a healthcare outcome.
Funding pathways that the Stellar integration unlocks
Stellar Community Fund: projects building real-world use cases on Stellar are eligible for grants. Healthcare supply chain with provenance and escrow is a strong application.
Stellar Enterprise Fund: enterprises deploying Stellar at institutional scale. Post-Series A Zytrak, operating across 5+ African countries, is a natural Enterprise Fund candidate.

IFC / BII / Norfund: DFIs with healthcare and Africa mandates who also have blockchain / fintech investment programmes. Zytrak's clinical outcome data + Stellar provenance records create a uniquely compelling impact investment case.
US State Department / USAID: precedent set by Zipline's $150M award. Healthcare supply chain infrastructure on blockchain aligns with both the US digital economy agenda and global health priorities.
Zytrak — Global Healthcare Procurement Built on Stellar | Platform Overview v2.0 | Confidential
